name: agent-cf-reviewer

criteria.name: issue_detection
criteria.description: Detects real bugs, security issues, and logic errors in the code

scoring.0: Misses all known issues seeded in the test code
scoring.1: Finds only obvious surface issues (formatting, naming) but misses logic bugs
scoring.2: Finds most significant issues but misses subtle bugs or security concerns
scoring.3: Finds all significant issues including subtle logic errors and security vulnerabilities

criteria.name: precision
criteria.description: Low false positive rate — reported issues are genuine problems

scoring.0: More than half of reported issues are false positives or misunderstandings
scoring.1: Several false positives that waste reviewer time to triage
scoring.2: One or two borderline false positives mixed with genuine issues
scoring.3: Every reported issue is a genuine concern — zero false positives

criteria.name: severity_accuracy
criteria.description: Issue severity ratings accurately reflect their real-world impact

scoring.0: Severity ratings are essentially random or all the same level
scoring.1: Some effort at severity but critical issues marked as minor or vice versa
scoring.2: Severity is mostly accurate with one issue mis-rated by one level
scoring.3: Severity ratings precisely match the actual risk and impact of each issue

criteria.name: actionability
criteria.description: Each issue includes a specific fix suggestion with file:line reference

scoring.0: Issues are vague observations with no file references or fix suggestions
scoring.1: Issues reference files but lack line numbers or specific fix suggestions
scoring.2: Most issues have file:line refs and suggestions but some are vague
scoring.3: Every issue has precise file:line reference with a concrete, implementable fix suggestion

notes: Multi-layer review should cover: correctness, security, performance, and maintainability. The reviewer agent includes the full 4-layer review methodology. For clean code, a concise 'no significant issues' is better than fabricated findings.
